description:
You are helping a machine-learning engineer organise a tiny sample of training data that already exists on the machine.

Current situation (already on disk):
1. Directory /home/user/raw_images/ contains four image files:
   • cat.jpg  
   • dog.jpg  
   • bird.jpg  
   • logo.png (should be ignored – it is not part of the training data)

Goal (what the automated test will verify):
A. A new directory hierarchy rooted at /home/user/dataset/ must be created with exactly one sub-directory named images  
   • /home/user/dataset/images/ will finally hold the three *.jpg files (cat.jpg, dog.jpg, bird.jpg) and only those three files.  
   • logo.png must remain in its original place (/home/user/raw_images/) and must NOT appear inside /home/user/dataset/images/.

B. A manifest file /home/user/dataset/train_manifest.txt must be produced with the following exact textual content (three lines, Unix line-endings, no trailing spaces, alphabetical order):

images/bird.jpg
images/cat.jpg
images/dog.jpg

C. A very small preparation log /home/user/dataset/prep.log must be created with exactly two lines (Unix line-endings, no trailing spaces) in this precise format:

Files moved: 3
Manifest created: train_manifest.txt

Instructions & constraints the agent must respect:
• Accomplish the job using a short shell script (around 3–4 commands is enough) executed from a Bash prompt.  
• Do NOT hard-code absolute paths in the manifest, only the relative path prefix images/ (as shown above).  
• Make sure the three .jpg files are moved, not just copied, so /home/user/raw_images/ ends up containing only logo.png.  
• The agent is free to display command output, but the final state of the filesystem must match parts A–C exactly, because the automated grader will check:
  1. That the directory structure and file placements are correct.
  2. That train_manifest.txt contains exactly the three lines shown, in that order, with nothing else.
  3. That prep.log contains exactly the two lines shown, in that order, with nothing else.
truth:
Prerequisites already present before the agent starts:
• Directory /home/user/raw_images/
• Files and their dummy (non-zero) contents:
  /home/user/raw_images/cat.jpg
  /home/user/raw_images/dog.jpg
  /home/user/raw_images/bird.jpg
  /home/user/raw_images/logo.png

Final state required for the task to pass:

1. Directory structure:
   /home/user/dataset/                (exists, permissions 755)
   /home/user/dataset/images/         (exists, permissions 755)
   /home/user/dataset/train_manifest.txt
   /home/user/dataset/prep.log
   /home/user/raw_images/logo.png     (still exists)
   /home/user/raw_images/cat.jpg      (must NOT exist)
   /home/user/raw_images/dog.jpg      (must NOT exist)
   /home/user/raw_images/bird.jpg     (must NOT exist)

2. File /home/user/dataset/images/ contains exactly three regular files with arbitrary binary data:
   cat.jpg, dog.jpg, bird.jpg

3. Exact contents of /home/user/dataset/train_manifest.txt (3 lines, final newline allowed):
images/bird.jpg
images/cat.jpg
images/dog.jpg

4. Exact contents of /home/user/dataset/prep.log (2 lines, final newline allowed):
Files moved: 3
Manifest created: train_manifest.txt
name: task_000000_f8cc06e3
